questions.question: A rectangular prism has length 5 cm, width 4 cm, and height 3 cm. What is its volume in cubic centimeters?
questions.explanation: Volume of a prism = length × width × height. V = 5 × 4 × 3 = 60 cm³.

questions.question: A cylinder has radius 2 inches and height 10 inches. Using π ≈ 3.14, what is the volume?
questions.explanation: Volume of a cylinder = πr²h. V = 3.14 × 2² × 10 = 3.14 × 4 × 10 = 125.6 in³.

questions.question: A cone has a circular base with radius 3 m and height 9 m. Using π ≈ 3.14, find the volume.
A sphere has radius 2 cm. Using π ≈ 3.14, what is its volume?
A triangular prism has a triangular base with area 12 square feet and height (length) of the prism 7 feet. What is the volume?
questions.explanation: Volume of a prism = base area × height. V = 12 × 7 = 84 ft³.

questions.question: If the volume of a rectangular prism is 120 cubic inches and its length is 8 inches and width is 5 inches, what is its height?
questions.explanation: Volume = length × width × height, so 120 = 8 × 5 × h. Therefore h = 120 ÷ 40 = 3 inches.
